title: Your Final Model Evaluation Checklist
concept: Evaluating a model requires checking for data leakage, choosing the right split strategy, and validating metrics before shipping.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the golden rule of time series cross-validation?

options.text: Always train on past data and validate on future data to avoid temporal leakage.
Always shuffle the dataset randomly before splitting.
Make sure the validation fold has exactly 50% of the total dataset.

exercises.explain: Correct! Never let future data leak into the past. Now, let's look at the complete checklist for model evaluation before shipping.

cards.title: Are we ready to ship?
cards.body: You've trained your model, tuned your settings, and the scores look amazing. But before you launch it to real users, how can you be absolutely sure your evaluation isn't lying to you? Let's go through the final pre-flight checklist!

cards.title: Rule 1: Leakage check
cards.body: The first item on your checklist is checking for data leakage. Ensure that scaling, normalization, and feature engineering were only fit on the training data, never the validation or test sets.

cards.title: The pre-flight inspection
cards.body: Just like a pilot inspects the wings, fuel, and controls before takeoff, a data scientist runs a checklist. Skipping a step could mean crashing the model once it meets real-world wind.

cards.title: Choosing the split
cards.body: Pick the split strategy that matches your task: Stratified K-Fold for rare classification classes, Time Series Split for time-ordered data, and Standard K-Fold for balanced, independent datasets.

cards.title: Is one metric enough?
cards.body: Never rely on a single metric. A model with great accuracy might have terrible precision. A model with great R-squared might make massive errors on outliers. Your checklist must require reviewing multiple metrics.

cards.title: Your Graduation! 🎉
cards.body: Congratulations on reaching Day 115! Let's celebrate how far you have come in your machine learning journey:

left.text:
What is data?
How do models learn?
Simple linear lines.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a critical step on the model evaluation checklist?

options.text: Verifying that no validation or test data was leaked during feature scaling
Ensuring the learning rate is set to exactly 1.0
Replacing all training data with synthetic random values
Verifying that the model achieves 100% accuracy on the test set

exercises.explain: Data leakage during preprocessing (like fitting a scaler on the entire dataset) is a common mistake that artificially inflates validation scores.

exercises.prompt: True or false: You should choose your cross-validation strategy based on the structure of your dataset (e.g. time-series vs. imbalanced classes).
exercises.explain: True! Matching your evaluation strategy to the data structure prevents leakage and ensures realistic performance estimates.

exercises.sentence: To make sure our model generalizes well, we check for data ___ and evaluate on multiple metrics before shipping.
exercises.explain: Preventing data leakage is the single most important rule of honest model evaluation.

exercises.prompt: Which of these workflows is ready for production deployment?

options.text: A model with stable metrics across Stratified K-Fold validation, with all scaling fit only on training splits, evaluated on a separate test set.
A model with 99% accuracy on a random split, where the scaler was fit on the whole dataset before splitting.
A model with a 95% training score and a 50% test score, where we adjusted settings to maximize the test score.

exercises.explain: A production-ready model has stable, un-leaked evaluation metrics across representative folds and test sets.

exercises.prompt: You have checked your model for data leakage, verified the split strategy, and confirmed that your validation scores match your test set performance. You are ready to ship! What is your final step?

options.text: Train one final model on the entire combined training/validation dataset using your chosen settings and deploy it!
Discard all your work and start over with random settings.
Deploy the K-Fold model that got the lowest score to be safe.
Train a new model using only the test set.

exercises.explain: You did it! Training on the full training dataset with the selected settings maximizes your data usage for the final production model. Congratulations on completing Day 115! 🎓🎉
